flashcards.front: What is the fundamental idea behind the Gradient Descent algorithm?
flashcards.back: To iteratively move parameters in the opposite direction of the gradient of the objective function, as this is the direction of steepest descent towards a minimum.

flashcards.front: Write the basic parameter update rule for Gradient Descent.
What is the key difference between Batch GD and Stochastic GD?
flashcards.back: Batch GD uses the entire dataset to compute the gradient for one update. Stochastic GD uses only a single, randomly selected data point to compute the gradient for each update.

flashcards.front: What is the role of momentum in enhancing Gradient Descent?
flashcards.back: Momentum accelerates convergence in the relevant direction and dampens oscillations by incorporating a fraction of the previous update vector into the current update.

flashcards.front: Name two common stopping criteria for the Gradient Descent algorithm.
flashcards.back: 1. The norm of the gradient is below a tolerance (||∇f(x)|| < ε). 2. The change in parameter values is below a tolerance (||x_{k+1} - x_k|| < ε). 3. A maximum number of iterations is reached.
